2603.18495.title: Cross-Domain Demo-to-Code via Neurosymbolic Counterfactual Reasoning
2603.18495.paper_summary: Recent advances in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have enabled video-instructed robotic programming, allowing agents to interpret video demonstrations and generate executable control code. We formulate video-instructed robotic programming as a cross-domain adaptation problem, where perceptual and physical differences between demonstration and deployment induce procedural mismatches. However, current VLMs lack the procedural understanding needed to reformulate causal dependencies and achieve task-compatible behavior under such domain shifts. We introduce NeSyCR, a neurosymbolic counterfactual reasoning framework that enables verifiable adaptation of task procedures, providing a reliable synthesis of code policies. NeSyCR abstracts video demonstrations into symbolic trajectories that capture the underlying task procedure. Given deployment observations, it derives counterfactual states that reveal cross-domain incompatibilities. By exploring the symbolic state space with verifiable checks, NeSyCR proposes procedural revisions that restore compatibility with the demonstrated procedure. NeSyCR achieves a 31.14% improvement in task success over the strongest baseline Statler, showing robust cross-domain adaptation across both simulated and real-world manipulation tasks.
2603.18495.paper_summary_zh: 最近在視覺-語言模型（VLMs）方面的進展使得視頻指導的機器人編程成為可能，允許代理解釋視頻演示並生成可執行的控制代碼。<lb/>我們將視頻指導的機器人編程定義為一個跨領域適應問題，其中演示和部署之間的感知和物理差異會引起程序不匹配。<lb/>然而，當前的VLMs缺乏重新構建因果依賴關係和在這種領域轉變下實現任務兼容行為所需的程序理解。<lb/>我們引入了NeSyCR，一個神經符號反事實推理框架，使得任務程序的可驗證適應成為可能，提供可靠的代碼策略合成。<lb/>NeSyCR將視頻演示抽象為捕捉基礎任務程序的符號軌跡。<lb/>基於部署觀察，它推導出顯示跨領域不兼容性的反事實狀態。<lb/>通過可驗證檢查探索符號狀態空間，NeSyCR提出程序修訂，以恢復與演示程序的兼容性。<lb/>NeSyCR在任務成功率上比最強基線Statler提高了31.14%，顯示出在模擬和真實世界操作任務中的穩健跨領域適應能力。
2603.18495.author: Jooyoung Kim et.al.
2603.18495.authors: Jooyoung Kim, Wonje Choi, Younguk Song, Honguk Woo
